metric_trajectory.purpose: Track how the primary test metric for each SD has moved across substrate iterations. Enables predictive hypotheses: given the co-variation of metric value vs substrate state, what should the next experiment produce? The trajectory is more informative than any individual PASS/FAIL because it reveals whether the substrate is the bottleneck or the hypothesis.

fields.primary_metric: Name of the key metric this SD should move
fields.primary_metric_description: What it measures and why it is the right quantity
fields.direction: lower_is_better or higher_is_better -- which direction indicates substrate progress
fields.target: Threshold expression the fully-implemented SD must satisfy
fields.observations: Ordered list of experiments that have produced readings on this metric, annotated with substrate state at the time. Chronological order. Include non-contributory and superseded runs -- the zero-readings and failures are the most informative part of the trajectory.
fields.current_blocker: What is currently preventing the metric from reaching threshold, or null if the SD is implemented and validated
fields.prediction: Explicit forward hypothesis: given the current trajectory slope and substrate state, what value range should the next experiment produce, and under what conditions? If null, no confident prediction is possible yet.

observations.metric_notes: Near bit-for-bit identical to 184158Z (same seeds, near-identical cosine values: 0.11914434 vs 0.11914417). 2/5 seeds pass C1. Confirms the collinear EMA substrate is the bottleneck, not noise or a specific machine/run artifact. The pattern is deterministic.

observations.metric_notes: PASS all 5 seeds. z_harm_a representationally distinct from z_harm_s; D3 reversal absent. Nonredundancy criterion met. Co-tested with SD-011 and SD-022 -- all three supports. First PASS for SD-019 on production substrate. Prediction confirmed: SD-022 substrate was the rate-limiting factor.

metric_trajectory.current_blocker: SD-022 substrate not active during EXQ-323. When z_harm_a derives from the same 50-dim proximity EMA as z_harm_s, the nonredundancy objective is applied to a pair of representations that share their information source. The training signal is therefore unstable: any reduction in cosine similarity achieved in one episode can be reversed when the proximity geometry changes. SD-022 (limb_damage_enabled=True) provides a causally independent information source for z_harm_a (body damage state), giving the nonredundancy objective a genuine basis for stable disentanglement.

prediction.expected_value: C1 passes 4+/5 seeds; cosine_sq_nonredundant < cosine_sq_baseline consistently; delta > 0.01
prediction.rationale: With SD-022 active, z_harm_a information source (body damage) is causally independent from z_harm_s information source (proximity field). The nonredundancy objective now works with genuinely separable representations. The co-variation across EXQ-241, EXQ-318, and EXQ-323 predicts that source independence is the rate-limiting factor for SD-019 consistency.
prediction.prerequisite_state: SD-022 limb_damage_enabled=True with harm_obs_a_variance > 0.001 (requires adequate hazard density or reduced heal_rate)

queue.title: Asymmetric per-mode hysteresis (Schmitt-trigger rails on SalienceCoordinator)
queue.implementation_hint: Per-mode enter_thresholds and exit_thresholds dicts on SalienceCoordinatorConfig. Trigger requires salience_aggregate > enter_threshold AND operating_mode[current] < exit_threshold AND argmax != current. Empty dicts reduce to legacy MECH-259 symmetric. queue.title: Invalidation trigger -- Phase 2(iv) of V_s runtime (BoundaryEvent subscriber)
queue.implementation_hint: InvalidationTrigger subscribes to MECH-288 BoundaryEvents and re-emits as graded BroadcastEvent (broadcast_strength = posterior * gain, NO binary thresholding). Phasic/tonic guardrail: rolling-mean over tonic_window past-tick aggregated posteriors; if estimate > tonic_threshold, current tick's phasic broadcast is suppressed (Aston-Jones & Cohen 2005, Clewett 2025 failure signature 2). Architectural commitment (V_s lit-pull verdict 3 option-c): trigger is a subscriber, NOT an independent comparator -- biological CA1/CA3 mismatch comparator collapses to subscription on MECH-288 boundary queue. Module: ree_core/regulators/invalidation_trigger.py.

metric_trajectory.primary_metric_description: Trigger re-emits graded posterior as BroadcastEvent; tonic guardrail suppresses next phasic under sustained high-activity period; verdict-3 dissociation: with event_segmenter lesioned, trigger never fires regardless of internal state.
metric_trajectory.target: 5/5 C1-C5 contracts PASS; 70/70 contracts + 7/7 preflight with flag OFF

observations.substrate_state: MECH-287 invalidation trigger landed 2026-04-22
observations.metric_notes: C1 backward-compat (None when off); C2 BoundaryEvent->BroadcastEvent strength=posterior*gain; C3 graded across [0.01..1.0] no binary threshold; C4 tonic guardrail suppress + reopen after tonic_window+1 quiet ticks; C5 verdict-3 dissociation (no segmenter -> no broadcast). End-to-end behavioural validation lives in V3-EXQ-476 combined cluster.

queue.title: Event segmenter -- Phase 2 of V_s runtime (hierarchical outer.inner BoundaryEvents)
queue.implementation_hint: EventSegmenter emits BoundaryEvent objects with nested outer.inner segment IDs at event-scale transitions. Canonical two-scale: fast=pe_threshold over (z_world,z_self) tau=1 min_seg=2; slow=bocpd_gaussian over (z_goal,) hazard=1/40 tau=40 min_seg=15. BOCPD via Adams & MacKay 2007 with Welford online variance + top-k pruning + underflow fallback. Hierarchical rule: slow forces outer+=1, inner=0 and suppresses same-tick fast event. Module: ree_core/hippocampal/event_segmenter.py.

metric_trajectory.primary_metric_description: Fast pe_threshold and slow bocpd_gaussian fire at correct z-score / posterior thresholds; hierarchical outer.inner correctness; min_segment_length suppression; force_boundary bypass.
metric_trajectory.target: 7/7 C1-C7 contracts PASS; 65/65 contracts + 7/7 preflight with flag OFF

queue.title: Backward trajectory credit sweep -- reverse replay at goal endpoint
queue.implementation_hint: Foster & Wilson 2006: reverse replay fires at reward endpoint during waking, concurrent with dopamine; credit propagates backward from goal to trajectory start. record_committed_trajectory(traj) at BetaGate elevation stores executed trajectory in _committed_trajectory_buffer (distinct from _exploration_buffer). backward_credit_sweep(outcome_quality) on BetaGate release sweeps backward; credit = outcome_quality * gamma^(T-1-t); ResidueField.update_valence(z_world_t, VALENCE_WANTING, credit). No-op when outcome_quality < backward_sweep_min_quality (default 0.6). Module: ree_core/hippocampal/module.py extension.

queue.title: Drive -> liking-stream -> approach cue bridge (weak reading)
queue.implementation_hint: Wires the missing link between SD-012 drive amplification, SD-014/SD-015 liking-stream substrate, and E3/BG action selection. Two integration sites: (a) update_z_goal() writes anticipatory liking pulse to VALENCE_LIKING at the GOAL location (NOT current z_world; distinct from update_liking() consummatory) gated on drive*z_goal_norm; (b) select_action() reads per-candidate goal_proximity, computes drive*proximity, negates (E3 lower-is-better), composes additively with dacc_score_bias before e3.select(). WEAK reading commitment: cue-side gain is function of drive * goal_proximity, not drive * residue.liking. Severed-bridge arm (cue gain=0) is the falsifiable test. Module: ree_core/regulators/mech295_liking_bridge.py.

metric_trajectory.primary_metric_description: Bridge fires anticipatory VALENCE_LIKING pulse at z_goal location; per-candidate goal_proximity drives negative score_bias (favours approach). Severed-bridge collapse (cue_gain=0) is the falsifiable signature for weak-necessity reading.
